questions.title: Q1. Compound Words vs Multi-word Expressions
questions.hint: When both are grammatically acceptable, which style should be preferred?

options.label: Prefer compound words whenever possible
options.prompt: - Prefer compound words over multi-word expressions when both are grammatically acceptable.

options.label: Prefer multi-word expressions whenever possible
options.prompt: - Prefer multi-word expressions over compound words when both are grammatically acceptable.

options.label: Decide case-by-case
options.prompt: - Decide compound vs multi-word expression case-by-case.

questions.title: Q2. Adjectival vs Noun-based Forms
questions.hint: When both forms are commonly used, which should be preferred?

options.label: Prefer adjectival forms
options.prompt: - Prefer adjectival forms over noun-based forms when both are commonly used.

options.label: Prefer noun-based forms
options.prompt: - Prefer noun-based forms over adjectival forms when both are commonly used.

options.label: Depends on hierarchy
options.prompt: - Choose adjectival vs noun-based forms depending on hierarchy.

questions.title: Q3. Use of Definite Articles with Anatomical Structures
questions.hint: When referring to body structures, should definite articles be used?

options.label: Use articles
options.prompt: - Use definite articles when referring to anatomical structures.

options.label: Avoid articles
options.prompt: - Avoid definite articles when referring to anatomical structures.

options.label: Depends on context
options.prompt: - Use or omit definite articles with anatomical structures depending on context.

rows: German: Störung der Entwicklung des Kindes

options.label: Prefer possessive constructions
options.prompt: - Prefer possessive constructions when expressing relationships.

options.label: Prefer prepositional constructions
options.prompt: - Prefer prepositional constructions when expressing relationships.

options.label: Depends on concept type
options.prompt: - Choose possessive vs prepositional constructions depending on concept type.

questions.title: Q5. Preferred Translation of Recurring SNOMED Terms
questions.hint: For recurring source words, should a single translation be enforced?

options.label: One approved translation per lexical item
options.prompt: - Enforce one approved translation per recurring lexical item.

options.label: Multiple accepted translations
options.prompt: - Allow multiple accepted translations for recurring lexical items.

options.label: Depends on context
options.prompt: - Choose lexical translations depending on context.

questions.title: Q5. Preferred translations for recurring terms
questions.hint: List preferred translations for recurring SNOMED terms (optional but recommended).

options.label: Technical medical terminology
options.prompt: - Use technical medical terminology.

options.label: Common clinical terminology
options.prompt: - Use common clinical terminology.

options.label: Mixed approach
options.prompt: - Use a mixed approach of technical and common clinical terminology.

options.label: Prefer eponyms
options.prompt: - Prefer eponyms over descriptive alternatives.

options.label: Prefer descriptive names
options.prompt: - Prefer descriptive names over eponyms.

options.label: Use both
options.prompt: - Use both eponyms and descriptive names as appropriate.

options.label: Depends on concept
options.prompt: - Choose eponym vs descriptive name depending on concept.

questions.title: Q8. Can Acronyms Be Preferred Terms?

options.label: Never preferred
options.prompt: - Never use acronyms as preferred terms.

options.label: Preferred if dominant in clinical practice
options.prompt: - Use acronyms as preferred terms when dominant in clinical practice.

options.label: Allowed only as synonyms
options.prompt: - Allow acronyms only as synonyms, not as preferred terms.

questions.title: Q9. Preferred Ordering of Clinical Descriptions

options.label: Prefer adjective-based ordering
options.prompt: - Prefer adjective-based ordering in clinical descriptions.

options.label: Prefer anatomical noun ordering
options.prompt: - Prefer anatomical noun ordering in clinical descriptions.

options.label: Depends on concept
options.prompt: - Choose word order depending on concept.

options.label: Follow national orthographic standards
options.prompt: - Follow national orthographic standards for capitalization.

options.label: Use SNOMED-specific capitalization rules
options.prompt: - Use SNOMED-specific capitalization rules.

questions.title: Q12. Consistency Preference
questions.hint: If several translations are equally correct, should AI:

options.label: Always use the same approved wording
options.prompt: - When several translations are equally correct, always use the same approved wording.

options.label: Prefer consistency but allow variation
options.prompt: - When several translations are equally correct, prefer consistency but allow variation.

options.label: Allow natural variation
options.prompt: - When several translations are equally correct, allow natural variation.

questions.title: Q13. Existing Translation Reuse
questions.hint: Before generating a new translation, should AI:

options.label: Reuse wording from sibling concepts whenever possible
options.prompt: - Reuse wording from sibling concepts whenever possible before generating new translations.

options.label: Reuse wording from parent concepts whenever possible
options.prompt: - Reuse wording from parent concepts whenever possible before generating new translations.

options.label: Generate independently
options.prompt: - Generate translations independently without reusing sibling or parent wording.

questions.title: Q14. Translation Validation
questions.hint: Should AI flag translations that violate established language policies?

options.label: Yes
options.prompt: - Flag translations that violate established language policies.

questions.title: Additional Language-Specific Rules
questions.hint: Please document any additional linguistic conventions important for SNOMED CT in your language that are not covered above.
